name: Advantage
definition: Gained during a Strike. You take the next turn, regardless of who initiated the Strike. If Advantage is gained multiple times during a Strike (even by multiple players), the last player to gain it goes next.

name: Before
definition: A trigger on many attacks that resolves at the start of your attack, before checking range to the opponent. It happens whether or not the attack hits. It does not happen if you are Stunned.

name: Hit
definition: A trigger on many attacks that resolves if the attack hits the opponent, but before the attack's damage is dealt.

name: After
definition: A trigger on many attacks that resolves after the attack would deal its damage. It happens whether or not the attack hits. It does not happen if you are Stunned.

name: Now
definition: A trigger on some Continuous Boosts that resolves immediately when you play the Boost. It marks which part of a Boost happens now and which happens later.

name: Cleanup
definition: The end-of-Strike step (and a trigger) resolved after both attacks. The attacker cleans up first, then the defender: resolve Cleanup triggers, discard non-sustained Continuous Boosts, put a hitting attack into Gauge (a missing one into discard), then resolve any End-of-Strike effects.

name: Power
definition: An attack's outgoing damage. You deal damage equal to your Power, reducing the opponent's life by that much (reduced by their Armor).

name: Speed
definition: Determines who resolves their attack first. The higher-Speed attack goes first and its player becomes the active player. On a Speed tie, the attacker becomes the active player.

name: Armor
definition: An attack's defense against incoming damage. Deduct Armor from the total damage you take over the course of the Strike. When not shown, treat Armor as 0. Armor is cumulative across all damage on the same turn.
examples: Focus has 2 Armor. Hit by a 7-Power attack, you reduce the damage by 2 and take 5.
notes: Armor prevents the first points of damage on a turn, but not damage dealt afterward on the same turn.

name: Guard
definition: An attack's stun resistance. If the damage dealt over the whole attack is greater than your Guard, you are Stunned. When not shown, treat Guard as 0. Guard is cumulative across all damage on the same turn.
examples: Focus has 5 Guard. Taking 5 damage does not Stun you (not greater than 5); taking 6 does.

name: Range
definition: Which spaces an attack hits, measured from the attack's origin (usually your character). Checked after Before effects; if the opponent is in range, the attack hits.
examples: A 3~8 range attack hits any opponent from 3 to 8 spaces away.

name: Attack Cost
definition: The cost to play a card as an attack (Gauge, Force, or nothing), shown in the card's top-left corner. It is paid at the Reveal step — not when you play the card's Boost.

name: Prepare
definition: An action: draw a card.

name: Move
definition: As an action, pay 1 Force per space to move (1 extra Force to move over the opponent). As a card effect, move the indicated spaces in either direction, switching sides with the opponent and continuing in the same direction beyond. Never count the opponent's space when resolving movement.
notes: Movement stops at the edge of the arena; you may not over-spend on movement effects.

name: Close
definition: Move toward the opponent the indicated number of spaces (or as many as possible) without switching sides with them.

name: Advance
definition: Move toward the opponent the indicated number of spaces (or as many as possible), switching sides and continuing in the same direction.

name: Retreat
definition: Move away from the opponent the indicated number of spaces (or as many as possible).

name: Push
definition: Move the opponent away from you, as if they were retreating.

name: Pull
definition: Move the opponent toward you, as if they were advancing.

name: Change Cards
definition: An action: spend 1 or more Force to draw that many cards. Spend all the Force before drawing any cards.

name: Boost
definition: The lower half of an attack card, used by taking the Boost action. Pay the Boost's Force cost, put it into your in-play area, and resolve its effect — do not pay the card's attack cost. Boosts are either Instant or Continuous.

name: Instant Boost
definition: A Boost that resolves its effect immediately, then is discarded.

name: Continuous Boost
definition: A Boost that stays in play, marked with a cross above its Force cost. Resolve its "Now:" effect, then add it to your Boost area; its effects are active until it is discarded (at a Strike or by an effect). There is no limit to how many you can have in play.

name: Strike
definition: An action (or Boost effect): set an attack face-down. The player who declares the Strike is the attacker; the opponent (the defender) then sets their own attack face-down. Then both attacks are revealed and resolved.

name: Exceed
definition: An action: pay the Gauge cost printed on your character card to flip it to its Exceed side, gaining your (usually stronger) Exceed ability for the rest of the game.

name: Reshuffle
definition: Shuffle your discard pile into your deck to form a new deck. This happens automatically the first time your deck empties and you draw or Wild Swing, or via the Reshuffle action. You get only one Reshuffle per game — if you must draw or Wild Swing from an empty deck after reshuffling, you lose the game immediately.
notes: Effects that shuffle your deck (without emptying it) do not use up your Reshuffle.

name: Force
definition: A resource spent to play Boosts, move, and pay effects. Generate it as needed by discarding cards from your hand or Gauge — 1 Force per Normal or Special Attack, and 1 or 2 (your choice) per Ultra Attack. You may only generate as much as you need; you cannot overpay.

name: Gauge
definition: A resource built when your attacks hit — the card enters your Gauge area during Cleanup. Spend Gauge to Exceed, to pay Ultra Attack costs, or discard it to generate Force.

name: Character Card
definition: Your fighter's card, with a Starting side and an Exceed side. It shows your Exceed cost, your character ability, and any special actions you can take.

name: Character Action
definition: A unique action some characters have, printed on their character card and beginning "As an action…". In the solo rules this is abbreviated "CA" and is especially common for Under Night (Season 6) fighters.

name: Normal Attack
definition: A basic attack card from the shared normal set that every character uses. Marked with a corner symbol.

name: Special Attack
definition: A character-specific attack. If it has a Force cost, you must pay that cost at the Reveal step or the attack is invalid.

name: Ultra Attack
definition: A character's most powerful attack, paid for with Gauge. Pay its Gauge cost at the Reveal step or it is invalid. An Ultra can generate 2 Force if spent for Force.

name: Regular Attack
definition: An attack formed by playing one card from your hand face-down.

name: EX Attack
definition: An attack formed by playing two cards with the same name from your hand face-down. EX attacks get +1 Power, +1 Speed, +1 Armor, and +1 Guard. Discard the extra copy when revealed.

name: Wild Swing
definition: An attack formed by putting the top card of your deck into play face-down without looking at it. Used when your attack is invalid, or by effects that make you Wild Swing.

name: Reveal Step
definition: Both players' attacks flip face-up. Discard the extra copy of any EX attack, and pay the Force cost of revealed Force Specials and the Gauge cost of revealed Ultras immediately — if you can't, that attack is invalid.

name: Invalid Attack
definition: An attack you can't pay for (or choose to invalidate from a Wild Swing). Discard it and Wild Swing, repeating until you reveal a valid attack. These replacement Wild Swings are not considered setting a new attack.

name: Priority
definition: Determines who resolves first in a Strike. The higher-Speed attack goes first (that player becomes the active player). On a Speed tie, the attacker is the active player.

name: Active Player
definition: The player whose attack resolves first (higher Speed, or the attacker on a Speed tie). They perform their entire attack — Before, Range check, Hit, After, then check for Stun — before the reactive player acts.

name: Reactive Player
definition: The player who resolves second. If not Stunned, they resolve their attack the same way the active player did; if Stunned, they skip their attack entirely.

name: Attacker & Defender
definition: The player who declares a Strike is the attacker; the opponent is the defender. The attacker sets their attack first, then the defender sets theirs.

name: Stunned
definition: If you take more damage than your Guard, you are Stunned: your attack does not activate (if you hadn't already activated it). A faster player can still be Stunned if the slower player hits back.

name: Stun Immunity
definition: You cannot be Stunned.

name: Sustain
definition: Prevents a Continuous Boost from being discarded when the current Strike ends. Sustaining lasts until the next Strike; you may keep sustaining a Boost across multiple Strikes.

name: Seal
definition: Remove a card from the game, placing it into your Sealed area.

name: Ignore Armor
definition: This attack treats the opponent's Armor as if it were 0.

name: Ignore Guard
definition: This attack treats the opponent's Guard as if it were 0.

name: Non-lethal Damage
definition: Damage that cannot bring the opponent below 1 life. For example, an opponent with 3 life dealt 5 non-lethal damage takes only 2.

name: Hand Size
definition: Your hand limit is 7. If you have more than 7 cards at the end of your turn, discard down to 7.

name: Golden Rule
definition: If any text effect contradicts the text of the rulebook, the card text takes precedence.

name: Critical
definition: A Street Fighter (Season 3) mechanic. Some attacks have a Critical effect you may pay 1 Gauge to activate when you set the attack. In the solo rules, Critical effects are treated as "set abilities."

name: Overdrive
definition: A BlazBlue (Season 5) mechanic. Certain characters build a separate Overdrive pile of cards and have an Overdrive ability that triggers under conditions printed on their card. See the specific character for details.

name: Astral Heat
definition: A BlazBlue (Season 5) character's signature finisher card. BlazBlue decks include their Astral Heat, and in the solo rules the AI starts with its Astral Heat in its deck.
